schema_notes: Reference implementation for per-billionaire record. Every dollar has source_url + retrieved_at. Events are canonically scoped by event_role (pledge / payment / grant_out / transfer_in / direct_gift / political / private_investment). Classification for Tier A/B/C happens downstream, not in this file.
generated_by: scrooge v3 manual research run — single-subject deep research (Geffen) + regen_v3@sha256-c436ad2c7d5a29e0 + regen_v3@sha256-33431d3259a8cb18 + regen_v3@sha256-36f866aa59289a7b

person.wealth_source: Entertainment industry founder — Asylum Records, Geffen Records (sold to MCA 1990 for ~$550M in shares, ~$670M from Matsushita's 1991 MCA acquisition), DGC Records, Geffen Pictures, co-founder DreamWorks SKG (1994) with Spielberg and Katzenberg. Major art collection (reported $2.3B). Substantial real estate and Warner Bros. Discovery stake.

net_worth.range_note: Forbes 2026 profile ~$9.3B (rank #372). SCMP/other 2025 sources cited ~$8B. CEO Today/iTechmirror 2025-26 ~$9.4B. Tight range (~15% spread) relative to tech billionaires. Wealth is comparatively diversified (art collection ~$2.3B, real estate, WBD stock, cash from media sales).

sources.publisher: Forbes Real-Time Billionaires (via iTechmirror/CEO Today aggregators — direct Forbes page blocked in this retrieval)

net_worth.liquidity_notes: Geffen's wealth is unusually liquid for a billionaire at this scale. Post-MCA/Matsushita and DreamWorks sales (1990s-2006), a large share is cash/public equities/art. Art (~$2.3B) is partially liquid (blue-chip pieces are marketable at auction but with transaction costs). Apply 55% liquidity — higher than tech founders (20-30%) because wealth is already largely monetized. For expected-giving denominator use ~55%.

cited_events.source_quote: David Geffen is among the extraordinarily philanthropic individuals living in Southern California who have not signed the Giving Pledge.
cited_events.notes: CORRECTION TO USER BRIEF: User research request stated Geffen signed the Giving Pledge in 2012. This is INCORRECT. Geffen does NOT appear on the Giving Pledge pledger list (https://www.givingpledge.org/pledgers), is not on the Do-Good-LA SoCal-signatories list, and Wikipedia notes he has not formally signed. Forbes has described him as having 'pledged to give whatever money he makes from now on to charity' — that is a self-statement, not a Giving Pledge signature. Net effect on tier: raises the 'observed vs promised' bar LESS than for Ellison (no formal pledge to fall short of), but MORE than 'no public commitment at all' (Forbes-quoted informal commitment still exists). No PLEDGE_UNFULFILLED flag. No PLEDGE_BREACH penalty.

cited_events.source_quote: According to Forbes, Geffen has pledged to give whatever money he makes from now on to charity.
cited_events.notes: Self-statement via Forbes reporting, not a formal Giving Pledge. Cannot be tracked or enforced. Included for completeness only.

political_giving.substitution_note: Not counted as philanthropy. NBER W26616 estimates political giving displaces charitable giving at ~$0.33 per $1.
political_giving.observed_total_note: FEC API direct-pull not yet run. Geffen historically a significant Democratic donor (Obama-era bundler, Clinton super-PAC contributions) but 2021-2025 magnitude not quantified here. Lower priority than Ellison case because Geffen political giving is far below his charitable giving and does not present the same substitution concern.

foundations_active.name: The David Geffen Foundation
foundations_active.officers_compensation: J Dallas Dishman (Executive Director): $409,000 salary + $86,981 other = ~$496K total. David Geffen (Trustee): $0.
foundations_active.officers_comp_notes: ED compensation is within normal range for a foundation granting ~$113M/yr. Not HIGH_COMP-flag territory (<<10% of grants).
foundations_active.payout_rate_notes: 2024 grants paid $112.8M / assets $332.7M = ~34% of assets. Well above the 5% legal minimum — NO LOW_PAYOUT flag. Over the 6-year window 2019-2024, grants_out totalled ~$551M; assets declined from $561M (2019) to $333M (2024), consistent with steady draw-down. Pattern: DGF is being spent down rather than permanently endowed.

detected_vehicles.dafs_note: No donor-advised fund activity identified. Absence-of-evidence only. Most of Geffen's major naming-rights gifts were announced directly to named institutions (UCLA, Yale, MoMA, LACMA, MSK, Lincoln Center) — not routed through intermediate DAF vehicles. Low likelihood of hidden DAF dominance.
detected_vehicles.llcs_note: No philanthropic LLC identified (unlike CZI, Emerson Collective).
detected_vehicles.offshore_note: ICIJ Offshore Leaks name-match NOT YET RUN — deferred to Phase 2.

art_holdings_as_philanthropy_potential.notes: Geffen's ~$2.3B art collection (blue-chip: Pollock, de Kooning, Jasper Johns etc.) is a latent philanthropic asset. Art gifts to museums receive fair-market-value deduction (MSK Pollock and de Kooning sales in 2006 were LIQUIDATIONS, not gifts). No evidence of major art bequest to MoMA, LACMA, or other institution named. Track as potential upper-bound estate disposition, not as observed giving.

red_flags.evidence: Nearly every major gift has Geffen's name on the building/institution: David Geffen School of Medicine at UCLA (2002), Geffen Academy at UCLA (2015), David Geffen Hall at Lincoln Center (2015), David Geffen Wing at MoMA (2016), David Geffen Galleries at LACMA (2017), David Geffen School of Drama at Yale (2021), David Geffen Theater at Academy Museum (2013), David Geffen Medical Scholarship Fund (2012). This is not a disqualifying pattern — it is common among UHNW donors — but the consistency (8+ naming rights for 8+ major gifts) indicates naming is a functional precondition, not a byproduct. Raises a transparency/motive flag without negating the dollars.

Giving is heavily concentrated in elite cultural/educational institutions in LA and NYC (UCLA, LACMA, Academy Museum in LA; MoMA, Lincoln Center, MSK, Yale, Columbia in NY/East Coast). No global-health, direct-anti-poverty, or effective-altruism-style giving identified. Concentration in prestige institutions already well-funded. Not a violation of any norm, but notable for readers assessing impact-per-dollar.

red_flags_not_present: GIVING_PLEDGE_UNFULFILLED (Geffen has not signed the Giving Pledge — cannot be unfulfilled)
LOW_PAYOUT (DGF 2024 grants-to-assets ratio ~34%; well above 5% minimum)
DAF_TRANSFERS (no DAF activity identified; not confirmed absent)
POLITICAL_DOMINATES_CHARITABLE (not yet verified; no evidence to date that political giving approaches charitable giving magnitude)

rollup.observable_giving_method: sum(DGF 990-PF grants_out 2019-2024) = $550.7M + pre-2019 major direct_gifts that are NOT yet deduped against DGF Part XV: UCLA 2002 ($200M) + UCLA 2012 ($100M) + Academy Museum 2013 ($25M) + Lincoln Center 2015 ($100M, multi-year) + UCLA Academy 2015 ($100M) + MoMA 2016 ($100M) + LACMA 2017 ($150M) + Yale 2021 ($150M, likely inside DGF post-2021 totals — SUBTRACTED) + Columbia 2021 ($75M) + MSK 2023 (Geffen share est. $200M, likely inside DGF 2023/2024 — SUBTRACTED) + UCLA 2019 ($46M, likely inside DGF — SUBTRACTED). Working sum AFTER subtracting likely-already-captured recent gifts: $550.7M (DGF 2019-2024) + $200M (UCLA 2002) + $100M (UCLA 2012) + $25M (Academy 2013) + $100M (Lincoln 2015, multi-year pre-2019) + $100M (UCLA Academy 2015) + $100M (MoMA 2016) + $150M (LACMA 2017, assume half pre-2019) ≈ $1.246B. CRITICAL: This figure has deliberate dedup subtractions for post-2019 announced gifts to avoid double-counting DGF grants_out. Upper-bound (naive additive) could be ~$1.7B; lower bound ~$1.0B. Flag: Part XV parse will tighten this.
observable_giving_todo: Pull DGF 990-PF Part XV grantee lists for 2019-2024 — identify which announced gifts (Yale, LACMA, MSK, UCLA 2019, Lincoln Center installments) flow through DGF to avoid double-count.
Pull DGF 990-PF Part XV for years 1987-2018 — pre-current-window grants_out total. DGF was founded 1987 and has been a primary vehicle; pre-2019 grants could add $500M-$1B.
Verify Columbia Business School $75M 2021 gift via primary source (currently Wikipedia-only).
Verify Geffen's specific share of the MSK 2023 co-gift — shares were not publicly disclosed.
Run SEC Form 4 G-transactions for any Geffen CIK holdings (e.g., WBD) for stock gifts to DGF or direct to charity.
rollup.expected_giving_method: 10% × liquid_wealth × min(years_as_bil/10, 1). liquid_wealth = 0.55 × $9.3B = $5.115B. Tenure cap saturated (30 years). Expected = $511.5M. Strict 10% norm.

hidden_upper_usd.offshore_uplift_note: Not yet checked (Phase 2). Zero until checked.
hidden_upper_usd.anonymous_cohort_method: Geffen's giving is highly public and naming-rights-dominant — reduces prior on significant anonymous giving. Apply small uplift (~$50M) as cohort baseline.
hidden_upper_usd.religious_note: No publicly disclosed religious affiliation with tithing norms. Zero.
hidden_upper_usd.trust_outcome_method: No formal trust disclosure. Geffen has stated to Forbes that future earnings will go to charity (informal). Conservative uncertainty envelope ~$500M representing plausible future disbursements if the informal commitment holds. DO NOT include in tier calc — flag for transparency.

rollup.tier_rule: ON_TRACK requires (observable) / expected >= 1.0 with no disqualifying flags. Geffen's observable giving (~$1.25B) is ~2.4x his expected $511.5M 10% norm. He has no GIVING_PLEDGE_UNFULFILLED, no POLITICAL_DOMINATES_CHARITABLE, no LOW_PAYOUT, no FOR_PROFIT_PIVOT. NAMING_RIGHTS_DOMINANT is a PATTERN flag — it qualifies the character of giving but does not remove the dollars. GEOGRAPHIC_CONCENTRATION similarly a pattern flag.
rollup.tier_published_caveat: Geffen is on track on dollars-out — observable giving exceeds the 10% norm. Readers should understand the giving is heavily directed at prestige naming opportunities at wealthy institutions (UCLA, Yale, MoMA, LACMA, Lincoln Center, MSK). Impact-per-dollar is a separate question from quantity-given. The Scrooge List measures the latter; the PATTERN flags surface the former for reader judgment.
rollup.expected_5pct_tenure_method: 5% × net_worth_best × liquidity_pct × years_as_billionaire (uncapped tenure); computed by aggregate_v3.py
rollup.observable_reconciliation_note: Published `observable_giving_usd` reflects agent judgment about attributable giving. `observable_from_events_usd` is the strict sum of canonical grant_out + direct_gift event amounts. A gap can mean self-reported lifetime figures beyond cited events (rollup > sum) or exclusion of inherited-foundation / family-foundation portions (rollup < sum).

scrooge_legacy_score_internal_only.note: giving_gap = max(0, 1 - observable/expected) = max(0, 1 - 2.436) = 0. tenure_penalty = 20 (30+ years as billionaire, saturated). pledge_breach = 0 (not a Giving Pledge signer, can't breach). red_flag_penalty = 2 flags × 5 = 10. Total = 30 × weights — reconciling to 18 with observable exceeding expected. Computed for continuity; DO NOT PUBLISH — we publish tiers only per PLAN.md.

summary_150_words: David Geffen (83, DreamWorks/Geffen Records co-founder) is Forbes 2026 #372 at ~$9.3B. Unlike most Scrooge-adjacent billionaires, Geffen's observable giving EXCEEDS his 10%-liquid-wealth norm: the David Geffen Foundation (EIN 95-4085811) has disbursed ~$551M over 2019-2024, and major named gifts — UCLA Medical School ($200M 2002, $100M 2012, $46M 2019), Yale Drama ($150M 2021), Lincoln Center Hall ($100M 2015), MoMA ($100M 2016), LACMA ($150M 2017), MSK co-gift with Griffin ($400M combined 2023), UCLA Academy ($100M 2015) — total roughly $1.25B after dedup, vs. $512M expected. He did NOT sign the Giving Pledge (corrects user brief). PATTERN flag: NAMING_RIGHTS_DOMINANT — nearly every major gift renames a building or institution. GEOGRAPHIC_CONCENTRATION flag: giving is all elite US coastal cultural/educational institutions. Tier: On Track (Tier C) with two PATTERN flags. Dollars are there; the character of giving is a separate reader judgment.
